items.title: Here's the Smartest Way to Invest in the S&P 500 in December
items.author: Stefon Walters
items.description: The article discusses the benefits of investing in a market-cap-weighted S&P 500 ETF, such as the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOO), as a simple and effective way to invest in the U.S. economy. It highlights the growth opportunities in the ETF's top holdings and the importance of consistent, long-term investing through dollar-cost averaging.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article recommends the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF as a good choice for investing in the S&P 500 index, citing its low expense ratio, active trading, and exposure to the top companies in the U.S. economy.
The article notes that the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust has a significantly higher expense ratio compared to the Vanguard and iShares S&P 500 ETFs, which can result in higher fees over time.
The article mentions the iShares Core S&P 500 ETF as another popular option for investing in the S&P 500 index, but does not provide a strong recommendation for or against it.
